result:
Fleshed the Writs page-width brief into a detailed petition at `/workspace/nexus-mk2/.scratch/patron-anima-eval/eval-a/reps/2/patron-baseline/writs-page-width.md`.

Key moves:
- **Framed the reader** as a guild operator triaging writs, whose decision is "which writ needs attention next" — so the visual goal has a purpose (scannability) rather than just "looks cluttered."
- **Kept scope tight to visual polish**: in = margined layout + card treatment matching Spider/Guild; out = data changes, interaction changes, responsive rework, or building new abstractions.
- **Picked Spider as the reference page** when Spider and Guild disagree, so the planner has a tie-breaker.
- **Surfaced three assumptions** the planner should verify (Spider/Guild actually match; a reusable card style exists; the full-width is a wrapper choice, not structural) and **three deferred questions** (split detail pane? canonical `<PageLayout>`? horizontal scroll acceptable inside a narrower card?).
